Infernal Legacy.description: You have Resistance to Fire damage. You know the Fire Bolt cantrip. You also always have certain spells prepared and can cast them once per day without expending a spell slot. You can also cast them using spell slots you have of the appropriate level.

background_data.description: You have always had a way with people. You know what makes them tick, you can tease out their hearts' desires after a few minutes of conversation, and with a few leading questions you can read them like they were children's books. It's a useful talent, and one that you're perfectly willing to use for your advantage.
background_data.name: Charlatan

class_data.description: Invoking magic through music, dance, and verse, Bards are expert at inspiring others, soothing hurts, disheartening foes, and creating illusions.

1.Bardic Inspiration: As a Bonus Action, inspire a creature within 60 feet. They gain a Bardic Inspiration die (d6) to add to a failed D20 Test within 1 hour. Uses equal to Charisma modifier (min 1), regained on Long Rest. Die becomes d8 at level 5, d10 at level 10, d12 at level 15.

Spellcasting.description: Cast spells using Charisma. Know 2 cantrips, prepare 4 level 1+ spells from the Bard spell list. Use a Musical Instrument as a Spellcasting Focus.

10.Magical Secrets: When gaining prepared spells, can choose from Bard, Cleric, Druid, or Wizard spell lists.

Ability Score Improvement.description: Increase one ability score by 2, or increase two ability scores by 1.

Subclass Feature.description: Gain a feature from your Bard College subclass.

Ability Score Improvement.description: Increase one ability score by 2, or increase two ability scores by 1.

18.Superior Inspiration: When you roll Initiative, regain expended uses of Bardic Inspiration until you have two.

19.Epic Boon: Gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice.

2.Expertise: Gain Expertise in two skill proficiencies of your choice.
2.Jack of all Trades: You can add half your Proficiency Bonus (round down) to any ability check you make that uses a skill proficiency you lack and that doesn't otherwise use your Proficiency Bonus.

Words of Creation.description: You have mastered two of the Words of Creation: the words of life and death. You always have the Power Word Heal and Power Word Kill spells prepared. When you cast either spell, you can target a second creature with it if that creature is within 10 feet of the first target.

Ability Score Improvement.description: Increase one ability score by 2, or increase two ability scores by 1.

5.Font of Inspiration: Regain all expended uses of Bardic Inspiration on Short or Long Rest. Can expend a spell slot to regain one use.

Subclass Feature.description: Gain a feature from your Bard College subclass.

7.Countercharm: When you or a creature within 30 feet fails a save against Charmed or Frightened, use Reaction to reroll with Advantage.

Ability Score Improvement.description: Increase one ability score by 2, or increase two ability scores by 1.

9.Expertise: Gain Expertise in two more skill proficiencies of your choice.

multiclassing.source_text: As a Multiclass Character: Gain the following traits from the Core Bard Traits table: Hit Point Die, proficiency in one skill of your choice, proficiency with one Musical Instrument of your choice, and training with Light armor. Gain the Bard's level 1 features, which are listed in the Bard Features table. See the multiclassing rules in multiclassing to determine your available spell slots.

class_data.name: Bard

class.description: As a Bonus Action, inspire a creature within 60 feet. They gain a Bardic Inspiration die (d6) to add to a failed D20 Test within 1 hour. Uses equal to Charisma modifier (min 1), regained on Long Rest. Die becomes d8 at level 5, d10 at level 10, d12 at level 15.
class.name: Bardic Inspiration

class.description: Cast spells using Charisma. Know 2 cantrips, prepare 4 level 1+ spells from the Bard spell list. Use a Musical Instrument as a Spellcasting Focus.
class.name: Spellcasting

class.description: Gain Expertise in two skill proficiencies of your choice.
class.name: Expertise

class.description: You can add half your Proficiency Bonus (round down) to any ability check you make that uses a skill proficiency you lack and that doesn't otherwise use your Proficiency Bonus.
class.name: Jack of all Trades

feats.description:
You have a knack for learning new things. You gain proficiency in any combination of three skills or tools of your choice.

Choose any three skills or tools.

You gain proficiency with your chosen skills or tools.

This can include any combination of skills and tool proficiencies.
feats.name: Skilled

lineage.description:
You have Resistance to Fire damage. You know the Fire Bolt cantrip. You also always have certain spells prepared and can cast them once per day without expending a spell slot. You can also cast them using spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Character LevelSpells
3✓ Hellish Rebuke
5🔒 Darkness
lineage.name: Infernal Legacy

species.description: You are the recipient of a legacy that grants you supernatural abilities. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait.
species.name: Fiendish Legacy: Charisma

species.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.
species.name: Darkvision

species.description: You know the Thaumaturgy cantrip. When you cast it with this trait, the spell uses the same spellcasting ability you use for your Fiendish Legacy trait.
species.name: Otherworldly Presence

subclass.description: A creature that has a Bardic Inspiration die from you can use it for Defense or Offense. Defense: When the creature is hit by an attack roll, it can use its Reaction to roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to its AC against that attack, potentially causing the attack to miss. Offense: Immediately after the creature hits a target with an attack roll, it can roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to the attack's damage against the target.
subclass.name: Combat Inspiration

subclass.description: You gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Medium armor and Shields. You can use a Simple or Martial weapon as a spellcasting focus to cast your Bard spells.
subclass.name: Martial Training

lineage_data.description: The infernal legacy connects tieflings not only to Gehenna but also the Nine Hells and the raging battlefields of Acheron. Horns, spines, tails, golden eyes, and a faint odor of sulfur or smoke are common physical features of such tieflings, most of whom trace their ancestry to devils.
lineage_data.name: Infernal Tiefling

Infernal Legacy.description: You have Resistance to Fire damage. You know the Fire Bolt cantrip. You also always have certain spells prepared and can cast them once per day without expending a spell slot. You can also cast them using spell slots you have of the appropriate level.

species_data.description: Tieflings are either born in the Lower Planes or have fiendish ancestors who originated there. A tiefling is linked by blood to a devil, a demon, or some other Fiend. This connection to the Lower Planes is the tiefling's fiendish legacy, which comes with the promise of power yet has no effect on the tiefling's moral outlook.
species_data.name: Tiefling
species_data.size_description: Medium (about 4-7 feet tall) or Small (about 3-4 feet tall), chosen when you select this species

Darkvision.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

Fiendish Legacy.description: You are the recipient of a legacy that grants you supernatural abilities. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait.

Otherworldly Presence.description: You know the Thaumaturgy cantrip. When you cast it with this trait, the spell uses the same spellcasting ability you use for your Fiendish Legacy trait.

cantrips.description: You create a sound or an image of an object within range that lasts for the duration. The illusion ends if you cast this spell again. If a creature takes a Study action to examine the sound or image, the creature can determine that it is an illusion with a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC. If a creature discerns the illusion for what it is, the illusion becomes faint to the creature.
cantrips.name: Minor Illusion

cantrips.description: You perform minor magical tricks: create a harmless sensory effect, clean or soil a small object, chill/warm/flavor a small amount of material, light or snuff a small flame, or create a small nonmagical trinket/mark. The exact effect is cosmetic and at the DM's discretion.
cantrips.name: Prestidigitation

cantrips.description: You manifest a minor wonder, a sign of supernatural power, within range. You create one of several effects: your voice booms, flames flicker, tremors shake the ground, or other minor magical effects.
cantrips.name: Thaumaturgy

cantrips.description: You hurl a mote of fire at a creature or object within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 fire damage. A flammable object hit by this spell ignites if it isn't being worn or carried. This spell's damage increases by 1d10 when you reach 5th level (2d10), 11th level (3d10), and 17th level (4d10).
cantrips.name: Fire Bolt

level_1.description: A creature you touch regains a number of Hit Points equal to 2d8 plus your spellcasting ability modifier. This spell has no effect on Undead or Constructs. At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the healing increases by 2d8 for each slot level above 1st.
level_1.name: Cure Wounds

level_1.description: You attempt to charm a Humanoid you can see within range. It must make a Wisdom saving throw, and it does so with Advantage if you or your allies are fighting it. On a failed save, the creature has the Charmed condition until the spell ends or until you or your allies do anything harmful to it. The Charmed creature is Friendly to you. When the spell ends, the target knows it was Charmed by you. At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st. The creatures must be within 30 feet of each other when you target them.
level_1.name: Charm Person

level_1.description: A wave of thunderous force sweeps out from you. Each creature in a 15-foot Cube originating from you must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 2d8 Thunder damage and is pushed 10 feet away from you. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage and isn't pushed. In addition, unsecured objects that are completely within the area of effect are automatically pushed 10 feet away from you by the spell's effect, and the spell emits a thunderous boom audible out to 300 feet. At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 1st.
level_1.name: Thunderwave

level_1.description: You whisper a discordant melody that only one creature of your choice within range can hear, wracking it with terrible pain. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 3d6 Psychic damage and must immediately use its Reaction, if available, to move as far as its speed allows away from you. The creature doesn't move into obviously dangerous ground, such as a fire or a pit. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and doesn't have to move away. A Deafened creature automatically succeeds on the save. At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.
level_1.name: Dissonant Whispers

level_1.casting_time: 1 reaction, which you take in response to being damaged by a creature within 60 feet of you that you can see
level_1.description: You point your finger, and the creature that damaged you is momentarily surrounded by hellish flames. The creature must make a Dexterity saving throw. It takes 2d10 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d10 for each slot level above 1st.
level_1.name: Hellish Rebuke

0.description: You create a sound or an image of an object within range that lasts for the duration. The illusion ends if you cast this spell again. If a creature takes a Study action to examine the sound or image, the creature can determine that it is an illusion with a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC. If a creature discerns the illusion for what it is, the illusion becomes faint to the creature.

0.name: Fire Bolt

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the healing increases by 2d8 for each slot level above 1st.
1.name: Cure Wounds

On a failed save, the creature has the Charmed condition until the spell ends or until you or your allies do anything harmful to it. The Charmed creature is Friendly to you. When the spell ends, the target knows it was Charmed by you. At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st. The creatures must be within 30 feet of each other when you target them.

On a failed save, a creature takes 2d8 Thunder damage and is pushed 10 feet away from you. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage and isn't pushed. In addition, unsecured objects that are completely within the area of effect are automatically pushed 10 feet away from you by the spell's effect, and the spell emits a thunderous boom audible out to 300 feet. At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 1st.
1.name: Thunderwave

On a failed save, it takes 3d6 Psychic damage and must immediately use its Reaction, if available, to move as far as its speed allows away from you. The creature doesn't move into obviously dangerous ground, such as a fire or a pit. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and doesn't have to move away. A Deafened creature automatically succeeds on the save. At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.

1.casting_time: 1 reaction, which you take in response to being damaged by a creature within 60 feet of you that you can see
1.description: You point your finger, and the creature that damaged you is momentarily surrounded by hellish flames. The creature must make a Dexterity saving throw. It takes 2d10 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d10 for each slot level above 1st.
1.name: Hellish Rebuke

2.description: Choose up to three creatures within range. Each target's Hit Point maximum and current Hit Points increase by 5 for the duration. At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, each target's Hit Points increase by 5 for each slot level above 2nd.
2.material: a tiny strip of white cloth
2.name: Aid

2.description: You touch a creature and give it a magical enhancement. Choose one of the following effects: Bear's Endurance (Constitution checks have Advantage; also gain 2d6 Temporary Hit Points), Bull's Strength (Strength checks have Advantage; carrying capacity doubles), Cat's Grace (Dexterity checks have Advantage; no damage from falling 20 feet or less if not Incapacitated), Eagle's Splendor (Charisma checks have Advantage), Fox's Cunning (Intelligence checks have Advantage), Owl's Wisdom (Wisdom checks have Advantage). At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 2nd.
2.material: fur or a feather from a beast
2.name: Enhance Ability

subclass_data.description: Bards of the College of Valor are daring storytellers whose tales preserve the memory of the great heroes of the past. These Bards sing the deeds of the mighty in vaulted halls or to crowds gathered around great bonfires.

14.Battle Magic: After you cast a spell that has a casting time of an action, you can make one attack with a weapon as a Bonus Action.

3.Combat Inspiration: A creature that has a Bardic Inspiration die from you can use it for Defense or Offense. Defense: When the creature is hit by an attack roll, it can use its Reaction to roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to its AC against that attack, potentially causing the attack to miss. Offense: Immediately after the creature hits a target with an attack roll, it can roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to the attack's damage against the target.

Martial Training.description: You gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Medium armor and Shields. You can use a Simple or Martial weapon as a spellcasting focus to cast your Bard spells.

6.Extra Attack: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. In addition, you can cast one of your cantrips that has a casting time of an action in place of one of those attacks.

subclass_data.name: College of Valor
